outbound_official.note: No official Guinea government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. Checked mae.gov.gn (Ministry of Foreign Affairs), paf.gov.gn (Police aux Frontières / DCPAF visa portal), gouvernement.gov.gn, service-public.gov.gn - all focus on inbound visa requirements for foreign nationals entering Guinea, not on where Guinean ordinary-passport holders can travel without a prior consular visa.

visa_types.name: Tourist Visa
visa_types.purpose: Short-stay visits for tourism and leisure purposes. Requires a valid passport, return ticket, and Yellow Fever certificate.
visa_types.notes: Applied online via paf.gov.gn; Entry Visa Letter received after approval (processing ~72 hours); physical visa collected at border arrival counter with biometric capture. Valid for stays not exceeding 90 days.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa (Visa de Transit)
visa_types.purpose: For travelers passing through Guinea in transit to a third country. Permits brief stay not exceeding three days.
visa_types.notes: Applied online via paf.gov.gn; collected at border arrival counter with biometric registration. Requires valid passport, onward ticket, and Yellow Fever certificate.

visa_types.name: Transborderment Visa (Visa de Transbordement)
visa_types.purpose: Long-validity cross-border movement visa, likely for frequent cross-border travelers or regional transit. Valid for up to five years.
visa_types.notes: Applied online via paf.gov.gn; collected at border arrival counter. Validity up to 5 years. Requires valid passport, return ticket, and Yellow Fever certificate. Individual stay duration per entry not specified on official portal.

visa_types.name: Long Term Visa (Visa de Long Séjour / VLS)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals intending to reside in Guinea for an extended period, typically for employment. Valid for one year from date of issuance.
visa_types.notes: Applied online via paf.gov.gn; collected at border arrival counter. Requires valid passport, copy of any prior visa, employment contract or equivalent supporting documentation, and Yellow Fever certificate. Non-ECOWAS foreign employers hiring foreign nationals must also obtain prior authorization from AGUIPE (Guinea's Employment Promotion Agency).

visa_types.name: Diplomatic, Service and Courtesy Visa (VDSC)
visa_types.purpose: For holders of diplomatic, service, or official passports and invited government guests. Issued upon prior official approval.
visa_types.notes: Requires a security code authorization from the relevant Guinea authority before online application can proceed. Duration and fee are governed by the official approval documentation. Requires valid passport, appointment letter, and Yellow Fever certificate.

visa_free.notes: Visa exempt per PAF e-visa portal country selector (code 2 = no visa required). Max stay duration not stated on official page.

visa_on_arrival.notes: Reclassified from visa_free to visa_on_arrival 2026-06-29. Rwanda MFA official page (minaffet.gov.rw) confirms the 2016 Rwanda-Guinea bilateral agreement provides: 'visa waiver for holders of diplomatic, service, or official passports, AND issuance of visas at entry points to the territory for holders of ordinary passports.' The 'issuance of visas at entry points' language indicates visa-on-arrival for ordinary passport holders, not unconditional visa-free entry. Per safety rule (classify DOWN when in doubt), moved to visa_on_arrival. Max stay duration not specified in the agreement text. The prior entry had no max_stay_days.

e_visa.notes: The PAF portal designates all countries not in the visa-free (code 2) category as requiring an e-visa. This includes USA, Canada, and UAE - previously recorded under visa_on_arrival with PAF 'code 1' designation, but re-classified to e_visa after 2026-06-27 re-verification: (a) US State Dept confirms US citizens must 'apply online' for a visa before travel - no VOA option; (b) official paf.gov.gn states UAE nationals require an e-visa; (c) secondary sources describing US/Canada 'code 1' as an extended-validity e-visa (up to 5 years) rather than a true visa on arrival. Visa types: Tourist Visa (USD 80, max 90 days), Transit Visa (USD 50, max 3 days), Transborderment Visa (USD 150, 5-year validity), Long Term Visa/VLS (USD 150, 1 year), Diplomatic/Service/Courtesy (VDSC, fee per approval letter). Process: apply online at paf.gov.gn/visa, receive Entry Visa Letter, collect visa at border arrival counter. Processing time: ~72 hours.

visa_policy.notes: Guinea operates an e-visa system branded as 'ETA Electronic Travel Authorisation of GUINEA' via paf.gov.gn/visa (Police aux Frontières / DCPAF portal). All 15 ECOWAS member states are visa-free (90-day max stay per APIP, re-confirmed 2026-06-29). Non-ECOWAS visa-free countries (Algeria, Egypt, Morocco, Namibia, Tunisia) confirmed via PAF portal code 2; max stay not stated. Singapore confirmed visa-free 30 days per Singapore MFA official advisory (re-confirmed 2026-06-29). Rwanda MOVED from visa_free to visa_on_arrival: Rwanda MFA official page confirms 2016 agreement provides ordinary passport holders 'issuance of visas at entry points' (VOA), not unconditional visa-free entry; diplomatic/service/official passport holders get full waiver (see conditional_access). USA and Canada require e-visa (US State Dept confirmed). The portal is maintained by the Ministry of Security and Civil Protection, Government of Guinea.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of Chinese diplomatic passports, service passports, and passports for public affairs (official) do not require a visa for Guinea for a maximum stay of 30 days. Agreement on mutual visa exemption effective 16 September 2017. Nationals holding ordinary Chinese passports endorsed 'for public affairs' are also covered for 30-day visa-free stay.

Holders of South African diplomatic, official, and service passports do not require a visa for Guinea for a maximum stay of 90 days. Based on bilateral visa waiver agreement signed in Pretoria on 24 November 2006, entered into force 23 January 2007 (UN Treaty No. 45066).

Holders of Rwandan diplomatic, service, and official passports do not require a visa for Guinea per the 2016 bilateral agreement. Ordinary Rwandan passport holders obtain visa on arrival at Guinea's entry points (see visa_on_arrival). Max stay not specified in the agreement text.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program found on any official Guinea government source. Searches of paf.gov.gn, mae.gov.gn, and related government domains returned no such program.

validation_notes: 2026-06-29 RE-VERIFICATION: Rwanda (RWA) MOVED from visa_free to visa_on_arrival. Rwanda MFA official page (minaffet.gov.rw) documents 2016 Rwanda-Guinea bilateral agreement as providing 'visa waiver for holders of diplomatic, service, or official passports, AND issuance of visas at entry points to the territory for holders of ordinary passports.' The 'visa at entry points' language is visa-on-arrival, not unconditional visa-free. Diplomatic/service/official passport exemption added to conditional_access. Safety rule applied: classify DOWN when in doubt.
2026-06-29 RE-CONFIRMATION: ECOWAS 15-member visa-free (90-day max stay) re-confirmed via apip.gov.gn and paf.gov.gn. No change.
2026-06-29 RE-CONFIRMATION: Singapore visa-free 30 days re-confirmed by Singapore MFA official advisory (mfa.gov.sg): 'Singaporeans may stay up to 30 days in Guinea without a visa.' No change.
2026-06-29 RE-CONFIRMATION: US State Dept re-confirms US citizens must apply online for e-visa; no visa on arrival. No change to e_visa default classification.

2026-06-27 RE-VERIFICATION: USA (USA), Canada (CAN), and UAE (ARE) REMOVED from visa_on_arrival and reclassified to e_visa (default). Basis: (1) US State Dept (travel.state.gov) confirms US citizens must apply online for a visa before travel to Guinea - no visa on arrival option exists; (2) paf.gov.gn official portal (per search result citing DCPAF) states UAE nationals require an e-visa, contradicting the Timatic VOA claim; (3) PAF portal 'code 1' appears to designate extended-validity e-visa (up to 5 years reported for US/Canada by secondary sources) rather than a true visa on arrival. Safety rule: classify DOWN when in doubt - these have been moved to e_visa.

2026-06-29 NOT ADDED: Tanzania (TZA) appears on embassies.net guinea-visa-exemption list but is absent from PAF portal visa-free list (Timatic/Wikipedia) and has no confirmation from any official Guinea source. Tanzania's own immigration portal does not list Guinea as visa-exempt. NOT added per official-source-first rule.
RETENTION: Non-ECOWAS visa-free (Algeria, Egypt, Morocco, Namibia, Tunisia) - kept; no new official source confirms or contradicts; max stay still unknown from official Guinea pages.
RETENTION: Diplomatic/service passport exemptions for China and South Africa unchanged.
RETENTION: Diplomatic/service passport exemptions reportedly exist for Romania, Russia, Zimbabwe, and Turkey per non-official aggregators - not added; no official confirmation found.
NO CHANGE: CBI, RBI, and fast_track remain empty - no official programs found.
PRIOR CORRECTION (previous run): Singapore max_stay_days updated from null to 30, confirmed by Singapore MFA.
PRIOR ADDITION (previous run): visa_types field populated with 5 official visa categories from paf.gov.gn.
gaps: Max stay days for non-ECOWAS visa-free nationals (Algeria, Egypt, Morocco, Namibia, Tunisia) not stated on any official Guinea page.
Rwanda visa-on-arrival: max stay duration not specified in the 2016 agreement text per Rwanda MFA page; fee unknown.

PAF portal 'code 1' meaning (assigned to USA and Canada, possibly others) is not explicitly documented on any accessible official Guinea page - secondary sources describe 5-year validity e-visa for US/Canada, unconfirmed from official Guinea sources.

UAE status remains ambiguous: Timatic reports visa on arrival (90 days, added April 2018), but official paf.gov.gn states e-visa required. Classified as e_visa per safety rule (classify DOWN). If UAE has a bilateral VOA agreement with Guinea, this would need an official Guinea government confirmation to re-classify.

Diplomatic/service passport visa exemptions reportedly exist for Romania, Russia, Zimbabwe (90 days), and Turkey (90 days diplomatic only) per Wikipedia and travel aggregators, but no official Guinea, Romanian, Russian, Zimbabwean, or Turkish government source could be confirmed. NOT recorded per official-source-first rule.
Tanzania appears on some non-official aggregator lists (embassies.net) as visa-free for Guinea, but not confirmed by PAF portal (Timatic/Wikipedia) or any official Guinea source. NOT recorded.
No RBI / golden visa / investor-residence program found on official sources.
No fast-track / talent / digital-nomad / startup visa program found on official sources.
No CBI program found on official sources.
No credential-based conditional entry rules found on any official Guinea government source.
Transborderment Visa individual stay duration per entry not specified on official portal; only overall validity (5 years) is stated.
Number of entries (single/multiple) not specified for Tourist Visa and Long Term Visa on official portal.
